source_artifacts.label: Scenario fixture
source_artifacts.note: The page hydrates from this JSON.

source_artifacts.label: Demo script
source_artifacts.note: Generates the underlying demo history that this fixture summarizes.

stages.meaning: This is the baseline most systems assume forever. Continuum treats it as only the starting point, not the whole story.
stages.summary: The community starts from a single recognized constitution. There is no institutional ambiguity yet.
stages.title: Root constitution

stages.meaning: Instead of pretending conflict never happened, Continuum keeps constitutional ambiguity visible inside institutional history.
stages.summary: Two child constitutions supersede the same parent, creating a branch conflict that replay can see.
stages.title: Competing branches

stages.meaning: Continuum does not reduce branch selection to admin fiat. The decision becomes a replayable governance object with explicit basis.
stages.summary: A constitutional proposal and a branch resolution choose branch B as the recognized path forward.
stages.title: Proposal-backed resolution

stages.meaning: This is a key Continuum idea: history can record an outcome before institutions are willing to treat that outcome as effective canon.
stages.summary: The branch resolution exists in history, but the chosen constitution is still not canonically active because execution proof is missing.
stages.title: Recorded, not effective

stages.meaning: Legitimacy is no longer only claimed. It is attached to a replayable execution event that future sessions can inspect.
stages.summary: A constitution_execution receipt is recorded for the resolution and its governing proposal.
stages.title: Execution proof

stages.meaning: This is the value proposition in miniature: institutions can change, but they change through visible, replayable history rather than silent replacement.
stages.summary: Replay now upgrades branch B into the active constitution, while preserving the conflict, proposal, resolution, and execution history.
stages.title: Canonical replay

summary: A community publishes competing constitutional branches, records a proposal-backed resolution, waits for execution proof, and then observes replay become canonically effective.
title: Constitutional conflict
